title: assets
description: manage your IoT assets and gateways

info.title: What is an Asset?
info.description: An Asset represents a physical or virtual IoT device in your infrastructure. Assets can be sensors, gateways, machines, or any connected device that sends or receives data through MapexOS.

templates.title: Template-Based
templates.text: Assets are created from templates that define their structure, data processing, and validation rules

protocols.title: Multi-Protocol
protocols.text: Support for multiple communication protocols including MQTT, HTTP, and LoRaWAN

organization.title: Multi-Tenant
organization.text: Assets belong to organizations with hierarchical structure support for complex enterprise setups

realtime.title: Real-Time Data
realtime.text: Assets send telemetry data in real-time and can receive commands instantly

location.title: Geo-Location
location.text: Assets can have geographical coordinates for location-based monitoring and analysis

warnings.noCertTooltip: MQTT asset has no active certificate. Open the asset and generate one in Authentication.

empty.title: no assets found
empty.description: it seems there are no assets matching your current search criteria or you don't have any assets yet.

messages.confirmDelete: are you sure you want to delete the asset "{name}"?

regenerateMqttToken.confirmBody: this invalidates the current device token. The device will lose connectivity until you re-provision it with the new credential.
regenerateMqttToken.success: new MQTT credential generated. Copy and re-provision the device.

password.label: password
password.hint: use Edit asset to set, rotate, or remove the password.

certificate.label: certificate
certificate.revokeConfirmBody: the device will lose MQTT access until a new certificate is issued.

dialog.title: generate MQTT certificate
dialog.warning: the private key is shown only once. Download the zip and store it securely - the platform never persists it. If lost, you must revoke this certificate and issue a new one.
dialog.replaceWarning: this asset already has an active certificate. Generating a new one revokes the existing certificate immediately.

revoked.title: revoked certificates
revoked.retentionNotice: retained 30 days for audit. Older records are archived.
revoked.empty: no revoked certificates for this asset.

healthMonitoring.title: health monitoring

mqttBanner.title: MQTT presence is the source of truth
mqttBanner.body: For MQTT-protocol assets, online/offline status comes directly from the broker (CONNECT / DISCONNECT). Health monitoring is always enabled — you only choose which route groups fire on each transition.

sections.heartbeatSourceDescription: How the platform learns the device is alive.
sections.detectionThresholdsDescription: When the absence of heartbeats is treated as offline.
sections.transitionRoutingDescription: Route groups fired when the asset goes offline or comes back online.

healthMonitoring.monitorOnlyHint: Leave both lists empty to monitor status only — no router action will be triggered. Status changes still update the badge and the connectivity history.

heartbeatMode.label: heartbeat source
heartbeatMode.implicitHint: Heartbeats are derived automatically from each data event the device sends. No extra integration needed.
heartbeatMode.explicitHint: The right channel depends on the asset's protocol — see the cards below.
heartbeatMode.mqttPresenceHint: Liveness is automatic via the device's MQTT connection — the platform detects connect and disconnect through the broker. Nothing to configure on the device beyond keeping the MQTT session open.
heartbeatMode.explicitHttpEndpoint: HTTP endpoint: POST /api/v1/heartbeat?ds=<dataSourceId> with body { "assetUUID": "..." } (same auth as /api/v1/events)

healthMonitoring.offlineRouteGroupsHint: route groups triggered when the asset stops responding
healthMonitoring.offlineRouteGroupsEmpty: no route groups configured for offline events
healthMonitoring.onlineRouteGroupsHint: route groups triggered when the asset recovers
healthMonitoring.onlineRouteGroupsEmpty: no route groups configured for online events
